tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying renal or urinary tract pathology, though no infection markers are present. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—this may warrant repeat testing or evaluation for hemoglobinopathies or anemia. Recommend nephrology referral and further renal workup.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
